Description: Free metasearch engine aggregating results from multiple search services and databases.
Evidence: Public implementation; active canonical repository; direct web research use case.
Notes: Accepted after verification of canonical repository and direct web research use case.

Description: Python perceptual image hashing module for similarity comparison.
Evidence: Maintained canonical ImageHash package; supports perceptual image matching workflows.
Notes: Accepted as the maintained canonical ImageHash package despite its historical fork relationship to photohash.

Description: Python face detection and alignment library.
Evidence: Public implementation; face detection and alignment are documented investigation-support capabilities.
Notes: Accepted as an image-analysis support library with a documented investigation use case.

Description: Python face recognition and facial attribute analysis framework.
Evidence: Public implementation documents verification, recognition, search, and analysis functions.
Notes: Accepted as an image-analysis framework with documented verification and search functions.

Description: HTTP toolkit for running high-volume probes against web targets.
Evidence: Active canonical repository; tagged OSINT; practical web asset reconnaissance use case.
Notes: Accepted as an active web asset reconnaissance tool.

Description: Secret scanner for Git repositories and other inputs.
Evidence: Public implementation; remote Git repository scanning and GitHub workflow support are documented.
Notes: Accepted for public repository exposure research; upstream states feature-complete maintenance mode.

Description: Finds, verifies, and analyzes exposed credentials across supported sources.
Evidence: Public implementation documents GitHub organization, user, repository, and related-source scanning.
Notes: Accepted for documented GitHub organization, user, and repository scanning.

Description: Browser library for rendering interactive node-and-edge network visualizations.
Evidence: Implementation is generic visualization infrastructure without a direct OSINT or investigative workflow.
Notes: Rejected: generic graph visualization library without a direct OSINT or investigative workflow.

Description: Extensible secrets scanner maintained by the original Gitleaks team.
Evidence: Gitleaks names it as the successor; supports GitHub, GitLab, Git, filesystems, and extensible sources.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging after Gitleaks identified it as the actively developed successor.

Description: Extracts email addresses, phone numbers, and social profile links from websites.
Evidence: Public implementation includes crawl, contact extraction, social extraction, and structured export modes.
Notes: Accepted as a focused website contact and profile extraction tool.

Description: Collects public TikTok profile metadata from a supplied username.
Evidence: Implementation-bearing fork with twelve commits beyond Dutchosintguy/TikTokOSINT; directly referenced by the supplied text.
Notes: Accepted as the implementation-bearing fork directly cited in the supplied text; fork relationship retained in metadata.

Description: Collects TikTok search, user, tag, and media data through SerpAPI and Apify.
Evidence: Public CLI and web implementation with documented SerpAPI, Apify, and yt-dlp collection workflows.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging as an active API-assisted TikTok collection project.

Description: Combines multi-target collection, entity correlation, graphs, and reporting in a desktop application.
Evidence: Repository contains a multi-module Python implementation rather than a landing page or stub.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging after implementation tree verification.

Description: Provides username scanning, social scraping, phone lookup, and search-query generation modules.
Evidence: Repository contains executable modules for username, phone, Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube workflows.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging after implementation tree verification.

Description: Collects exposed email addresses from supported social platforms and checks related leak data.
Evidence: Public Rust implementation documents Instagram, LinkedIn, X/Twitter, and PwnDB-related workflows.
Notes: Accepted as a cross-platform social email collection tool; leak-source use requires operator policy review.

Description: Downloads image galleries and media collections from supported websites.
Evidence: GitHub README identifies Codeberg as the canonical active-development repository; implementation and current commits verified there.
Notes: Accepted using the canonical Codeberg repository identified by the project's GitHub migration notice.

Description: Collects X timelines and search results through Playwright with resume and proxy controls.
Evidence: Public implementation matches the feature description in the supplied GitHub Topics reconnaissance.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging as the repository matching the supplied GitHub Topics description.

Description: Collects public Twitter data through the frontend API without official API authentication.
Evidence: Canonical high-adoption repository matching the ambiguous twitter-scraper name; archived but historically relevant.
Notes: Accepted as the canonical high-adoption repository matching the text; archived status is explicit.

Description: Provides an unofficial Python library for collecting Twitter posts and user records.
Evidence: Public implementation supports searches by phrase and ID plus user collection; project is stale but not archived.
Notes: Accepted as a historical implementation; stale update date remains visible.

Description: Collects public Twitter posts, profiles, and account relationships without the official API.
Evidence: Widely used historical OSINT implementation; repository is archived and retained for reference.
Notes: Accepted for historical OSINT relevance with archived status explicit.

Description: Investigates usernames, email addresses, phone numbers, and names without mandatory API keys.
Evidence: Small but implementation-bearing multi-target OSINT project with documented confidence scoring.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging as a small implementation-bearing multi-target project.

Description: Searches phone-number variations across search engines and correlates identity clues.
Evidence: Public Python implementation documents multi-engine phone search and correlation.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging as a focused phone-number investigation project.

Description: Searches phone numbers across public engines and people-search sources in a Rust CLI.
Evidence: Distinct Rust implementation from the same maintainer with a documented phone OSINT workflow.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging as a distinct Rust phone-number investigation implementation.

Description: Curated list of username-search tools and online services.
Evidence: Repository is explicitly a curated source list without its own checker implementation.
Notes: Rejected: curated link and resource list without its own checker implementation.

Description: Curated resources and guidance for Discord investigations.
Evidence: Repository contains only README.md and LICENSE, without an implementation-bearing tool.
Notes: Rejected: resource list containing only README.md and LICENSE, without a tool implementation.

Description: Exports accessible Discord message history and rich media to local files.
Evidence: Active implementation with desktop, CLI, container, structured export, and documented bot-token guidance.
Notes: Accepted as an active evidence-preservation tool; bot access is preferable because user-account automation may violate Discord terms.

Description: Saves accessible Discord chat history for offline preservation and review.
Evidence: Active desktop application, browser script, and offline viewer with substantial source implementation.
Notes: Accepted as an active Discord evidence-preservation application.

Description: Provides user, guild, application, invite, and snowflake lookup functions.
Evidence: Substantial self-hostable lookup implementation; archived status retained explicitly.
Notes: Accepted for lookup utility and historical relevance with archived status explicit.

Description: Retrieves public user, server, and application metadata from Discord identifiers.
Evidence: Small implementation-bearing ID lookup tool with documented public-data outputs.
Notes: Accepted into Discord and Emerging as a small implementation-bearing public-data lookup tool.

Description: Extracts URLs and media references from supported Discord archive formats.
Evidence: Public Rust implementation supports Discord History Tracker, discard2, and DiscordChatExporter inputs.
Notes: Accepted as an evidence-analysis helper for multiple Discord archive formats.

Description: Creates high-fidelity local archives of accessible Discord content.
Evidence: Implementation-bearing archival tool with explicit archived repository status.
Notes: Accepted for historical archival value with archived status explicit.

Description: Runs face detection, recognition, matching, and broader human analysis in browsers and Node.js.
Evidence: Substantial TypeScript implementation includes face ID, face matching, image, video, and Node.js workflows.
Notes: Accepted as an active face-analysis library with direct image investigation and comparison use cases.

Description: Provides browser and Node.js face detection, recognition, description, and attribute analysis.
Evidence: Implementation-bearing TensorFlow.js library with bundled recognition models; archived status retained.
Notes: Accepted for historical and practical face-analysis value with archived status explicit.

Description: Organizes and searches local photo collections using faces, objects, locations, and visual attributes.
Evidence: Active self-hosted implementation contains dedicated face classification, embedding, matching, and clustering modules.
Notes: Accepted as an active local evidence and photo-collection analysis platform.

Description: Self-hosted NVR and computer-vision platform for camera monitoring.
Evidence: Self-hosted implementation supports local camera monitoring with motion, object, face, and license-plate detection.
Notes: Accepted after scope clarification that CCTV and online-camera monitoring are valid intelligence collection and analysis workflows.

Description: AI camera and NVR platform for local CCTV and home-security monitoring.
Evidence: Implementation provides local camera analytics, face recognition, re-identification, VLM analysis, and pluggable AI skills.
Notes: Accepted after scope clarification that camera monitoring and video intelligence belong in the catalogue.

Description: Local-first platform for security-camera viewing, recording, and detection.
Evidence: Implementation provides local recording, monitoring, on-device detection, and semantic video-search workflows.
Notes: Accepted after scope clarification that CCTV and online-camera monitoring are valid intelligence workflows.

Description: Browser extension that sends an image to multiple reverse-image search engines.
Evidence: Active cross-browser implementation for Chrome, Edge, and Safari with multiple image-search engines.
Notes: Accepted as an active multi-engine reverse-image search extension with an explicit GPL license.

Description: Desktop reverse-image search client for several image and media-matching services.
Evidence: Substantial active implementation, but the repository does not declare an open-source license.
Notes: Rejected because the public repository does not declare an open-source license.

Description: Searches for social profiles related to a supplied face image.
Evidence: Implementation combines face recognition with reverse-image and social-profile search workflows.
Notes: Accepted as an implementation-bearing face-led reverse-search tool; results require manual identity verification.

Description: Command-line client for Google reverse-image search.
Evidence: Active MIT-licensed CLI with a direct reverse-image search workflow.
Notes: Accepted as an active, focused, MIT-licensed reverse-image search CLI.

Description: Searches a local image collection with image or text queries using CLIP embeddings.
Evidence: Public implementation provides both image-to-image and text-to-image retrieval over local collections.
Notes: Accepted for local image-to-image and text-to-image retrieval; last update remains visible in metadata.

Description: Performs local similar-image retrieval and duplicate-image detection.
Evidence: Licensed implementation supports image-by-image retrieval and deduplication without an external search service.
Notes: Accepted as a licensed local reverse-image retrieval and deduplication tool.

Description: Indexes local photos and videos for similarity, face, and semantic discovery.
Evidence: Active self-hosted implementation documents reverse-image lookup and similar-face search over local collections.
Notes: Accepted as an active self-hosted visual-evidence search platform.

Description: Recognizes and compares faces from Python or the command line.
Evidence: Widely used active implementation exposes both a Python API and command-line face comparison workflow.
Notes: Accepted as a widely used MIT-licensed face comparison implementation.

Description: Self-hosted face recognition and verification service with a REST API.
Evidence: Substantial licensed implementation provides recognition, verification, detection, and similarity services.
Notes: Accepted as a substantial Apache-licensed self-hosted face-analysis service.

Description: Re-identifies people across images and camera views with pretrained models and training tools.
Evidence: Active MIT-licensed person re-identification toolbox with pretrained models and image/video workflows.
Notes: Accepted for cross-camera person re-identification workflows.

Description: Provides person re-identification models, training pipelines, and inference tooling.
Evidence: Licensed implementation covers person re-identification and supports investigation-oriented cross-camera matching.
Notes: Accepted as an Apache-licensed cross-camera re-identification toolbox.

Description: Records and analyzes local IP-camera streams with real-time object detection and search.
Evidence: Highly active local NVR implementation with object detection, tracking, recording, and searchable event workflows.
Notes: Accepted as a highly active local NVR and video-intelligence platform.

Description: Monitors, records, and reviews IP, USB, and analog camera feeds.
Evidence: Long-running active CCTV platform with substantial source implementation and broad camera support.
Notes: Accepted as an active open-source CCTV platform with broad camera support.

Description: Provides a web interface for camera monitoring, motion detection, recording, and review.
Evidence: Active licensed web frontend for the motion daemon with practical multi-camera monitoring workflows.
Notes: Accepted because camera monitoring and evidence review are within the clarified intelligence scope.

Description: Runs local video surveillance, recording, motion analysis, and event capture for camera streams.
Evidence: Active MIT-licensed surveillance agent supports RTSP camera feeds and isolated local deployments.
Notes: Accepted as an active MIT-licensed local surveillance agent.

Description: Records camera streams and applies local object detection through a web video-management interface.
Evidence: Licensed GitHub mirror of an implementation-bearing VMS with continuous recording and TensorFlow Lite detection.
Notes: Accepted as a licensed implementation-bearing GitHub mirror; the canonical project is hosted on Codeberg.

Description: Adds object detection, tracking, notifications, summaries, and search to security-camera feeds.
Evidence: Rapidly developed licensed implementation turns RTSP feeds into searchable detection events.
Notes: Accepted as an active licensed video-analysis and search platform.

Description: Uses vision-language models to monitor camera feeds and search recorded frames with natural language.
Evidence: New licensed camera platform with relay nodes, private RTSP access, chat, and semantic video search.
Notes: Accepted into Image and Emerging because the implementation is licensed and relevant but still comparatively new.

Description: Provides privacy-focused local camera recording and monitoring without a cloud dependency.
Evidence: Very new but active licensed NVR implementation with local-first operation and practical recording workflows.
Notes: Accepted into Image and Emerging as a very new but active local-first NVR.

Discovery Source: Independent GitHub research: online camera intelligence
Description: Locates publicly accessible IP-camera streams using public directories and web-search queries.
Evidence: Implementation combines Insecam directory collection, search queries, GeoIP enrichment, and stream-type verification; highly dual-use and still new.
Notes: Accepted into Image and Emerging as a dual-use public-camera discovery tool; use must remain lawful and authorized.

Discovery Source: Independent GitHub research: online camera intelligence
Description: Assesses camera services for exposed ports, interfaces, streams, and configuration weaknesses.
Evidence: Implementation includes active probing and default-credential testing, which goes beyond passive open-source collection.
Notes: Rejected from the OSINT catalogue because active probing and default-credential testing go beyond passive open-source collection.

Discovery Source: Independent GitHub research: online camera intelligence
Description: Scrapes a public camera directory and runs object detection over captured frames.
Evidence: Direct public-camera analysis implementation, but it has not been updated since 2018 and has been superseded by newer projects.
Notes: Rejected because it has not been updated since 2018 and newer maintained public-camera discovery tools cover the workflow.

Discovery Source: Independent GitHub research: online camera intelligence
Description: Discovers and views reachable IoT camera services.
Evidence: Implementation is relevant to camera reconnaissance, but the repository does not declare an open-source license.
Notes: Rejected because the repository does not declare an open-source license.

Discovery Source: Independent GitHub research: online camera intelligence
Description: Maps public traffic, weather, and webcam feeds with local vision analysis.
Evidence: Potentially relevant concept, but the repository is an unlicensed initial release with minimal adoption and history.
Notes: Rejected for now because it is an unlicensed initial release with insufficient implementation history and adoption.

Description: Implements face detection, alignment, recognition, embeddings, and cross-platform analysis workflows.
Evidence: Active implementation with Python, C++, and desktop tooling; README states MIT for code and separate non-commercial or negotiated terms for supplied models.
Notes: Accepted with explicit metadata distinction: source code is MIT, while supplied models have separate non-commercial or negotiated license terms.
